title: lathyrus sylvestris subsp. ensifolius
intro: Lathyrus sylvestris subsp. ensifolius, also known as the hairy vetch, is a perennial legume plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lathyrus sylvestris subsp. ensifolius include its ability to support digestive health, reduce anxiety and stress, and lower blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using its roots and seeds to create a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety.
